basis: CIF NWE / Dated Brent physical proxy
note: Brent has jumped back above $79 (WTI ~$74) as the U.S.–Iran conflict escalated over the weekend, reinserting the war-risk premium the June truce had drained. Iran declared the Strait of Hormuz closed 'until further notice' after striking a container ship; the U.S. answered with further strikes; just six vessels transited on Sunday, a five-week low, and Monday's traffic was near-nonexistent, most crossing dark. Physical NWE differentials are firming with the benchmark, and refined-product (diesel and jet) tightness is acute — European diesel margins hit a record after Russia's export ban. Editorial proxy, not a traded quote — indicative, and watch tanker-transit data over the rhetoric.
